body: Um, actually, while Weeping Angels prefer to send their victims back in time as they do in Blink, they can and often do in other episodes kill their victims instantly on contact if they are scared or have some use for a human consciousness. Such is the case in the two-part story The Time of Angels and Flesh and Stone, in which anyone caught by the Angels in either episode have their necks snapped. Furthermore, Village of the Angels introduces the idea that if you are sent back in time by an Angel twice in a row, it will kill you, though I wouldn't blame you for ignoring this because the show isn't consistent about it: Rory Williams gets sent back in time twice in a row in The Angels Take Manhattan and is perfectly fine beyond the standard 'dying of old age' thing humans tend to do. Doctor Who lore is very inconsistent.
The Elk Rotary is advertised later in the show, just not in the gardening scene
Um, actually, Dovahkiin (not Dohavkin) means dragonborn, and that's still a title/role. The player character is unnamed.
Judas isn’t discovered before the final layer, since he is first seen in Satan’s mouth.

Um, actually, a lot (most) of the Beastars story takes place in the city outside of the academy. The story starts at the academy and it does remain a core setting, but as the story progresses, both Legosi and Louis move out of the academy and into the city proper, as the focus moves to Louis' involvement with the mafia
um actually there are also other vaults without an experiment labeled "control vaults"
Achilles is still encountered in the biomes multiple times, when he's talking to Patroclus.
this isn't wrong but it is just copied from the um actually musical episode, which feels like not the point of this site.
The ship is not called "The Serenity", just "Serenity".  Inara corrects an alliance official of this in "Bushwacked."
Um actually chests don't look different depending on the wood.

Um actually, Sally Sparrow doesn't discover the message on her DVD, rather another character (Larry Nightingale) points out that this Easter Egg is on several DVD film releases, that turn out to be exactly the films Sally owns DVDs of.
Seth Rollins won Money in the Bank twice, 2014 and 2025.

Um, actually, Homestar shouldn't probably look up what that word means, he should REALLY look up what that word means. Also, I would rephrase it from "whom Homestar calls his 'best friend and confidant' and the town hunk" to "the town hunk, and whom Homestar calls his 'best friend and confidant'" for clarity's sake.
742 Evergreen terrace is by far,  the most consistent address of the simpsons house. all the others were just in the early seasons when the writers were still figuring it out

Whoops, I wrote the wrong word down. They're not called the Ottomans in Age of Empires II, they're called the Turks
